title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board featuring the Coral NPU accelerator for on-device AI inference. It runs Gemma models and ships with integrated I/O including screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs for edge AI experimentation.
writeup: This video from Google for Developers covers running Gemma models on the Coral Board, a low-power development board built around the Coral NPU accelerator for on-device AI inference. The board ships with integrated I/O including a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, supporting edge experiments such as facial recognition, machine translation, and vision-language tasks. The relevance is local inference: running models directly on hardware rather than in the cloud, with the accelerator handling the workload at low power.
